title: Apply for a firearms dealer, gunsmith or collector’s licence
description: Apply for a licence to operate as a firearms dealer, a gunsmith or a collector.
summary: If you want to sell firearms, repair them as a gunsmith, or hold a registered collection, you need a special licence under Section 6 of the Firearms Act. This is Form C.
There are two parts to the form. Part A is for individuals applying in their own name. Part B is for companies. If a company is applying, every director and partner needs to be named.
Police record certificates are required for every named applicant. You also have to show that your premises and storage are approved by The Royal Barbados Police Force.
This is the most rigorous of the firearms licences. Decisions usually take longer than for a single-firearm licence because the Police have to check every person and every storage location.
who_can_apply: an individual over 21 who wants to be a registered dealer, gunsmith or collector
a company that wants to deal in firearms

items: your full name (or the company’s name)
your business address and storage location
the type of activity (dealer, gunsmith or collector)
the names of every director or partner of the company
the address of every place you will store firearms

items.name: Two passport-size photographs of every applicant
Police record certificate for every applicant
Proof of payment of the application fee

fees: Fees vary by category. Pay at the Barbados Revenue Authority.
how_long: A decision usually takes 10 to 14 weeks.

options.heading: In person
content: Hand the completed Form C Application for a Firearms Dealer, Gunsmith or Collector’s Licence in to Police Headquarters, Roebuck Street, St. Michael.

contact.address:
Police Headquarters
Roebuck Street
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘21 or over’ minimum age is inferred from common dealer-licence practice. The form does not state a minimum age.
‘10 to 14 weeks’ processing time is an estimate.
Document list is inferred from common practice for dealer applications.
